commit.message:
Initial foundry-networking repository structure

- scenarios/scenarios.md: 12 networking test scenarios with Mermaid diagrams
  covering inbound (PE, IP filtering), outbound (managed VNet, BYO VNet,
  managed PE, hub-spoke), hybrid (VPN Gateway, App Gateway), and infra
  (DNS validation, trusted services)
- infra/main.bicep: orchestrator deploying VNet, Foundry, Storage, Cosmos DB,
  AI Search, Key Vault, Private Endpoints, DNS zones, Bastion + test VM
- infra/modules/: 9 reusable Bicep modules (vnet, foundry, storage, cosmosdb,
  ai-search, keyvault, private-endpoint, private-dns-zone, bastion,
  log-analytics)
- infra_deploy.ps1: main deployment script with provider registration, RG
  creation, Bicep deployment, RBAC, and VM access configuration
- lab_start.ps1 / lab_stop.ps1: start/stop lab resources to manage costs
- foundry_info.ps1: display lab resource status and networking config
- get_logs.ps1: retrieve diagnostic logs from Log Analytics

perf: pre-compute quoted column names for DataTable.Update RowUpdating handler

The RowUpdating event fires once per INSERT row during DataTable.Update().
Previously the handler iterated all columns, called commandBuilder.QuoteIdentifier
for every refreshable column, and allocated a ResizeArray on every row.

Column metadata (AutoIncrement/AllowDBNull flags) and quoted column names are
constant for the lifetime of a single Update() call, so we pre-compute them
once before subscribing:

- alwaysRefreshQuoted: quoted names for AutoIncrement columns (server always
  supplies the value)
- maybeRefreshCols: (DataColumn, quotedName) pairs for AllowDBNull columns;
  only added per-row when the row has no explicit value

This eliminates O(columns) QuoteIdentifier calls and a full column-iteration
per INSERT row, replacing them with a single pre-computation and a cheaper
per-row scan over the smaller maybeRefreshCols array.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: migrate figure serving to Supabase Storage

Figures are generated by the scientific pipeline and should NOT be
committed to git. This commit:

- Removes all 467 tracked binary figures from the git index
  (files remain on disk but are no longer tracked)
- Adds figures/**/*.png|jpg|svg|pdf to .gitignore to prevent
  future accidental commits
- Implements Supabase Storage support in figureUrl() utility:
 returns Supabase
      public URL (production mode, no files in git needed)
 falls back to /api/figures route (local dev mode,
      reads from filesystem)
- Adds scripts/web/upload_figures_to_supabase.py to sync generated
  figures from local disk to Supabase Storage bucket 'figures'
- Updates web/.env.example with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL
- Rewrites web/DEPLOYMENT.md with full Supabase Storage workflow

To restore figures in production:
  1. Create 'figures' bucket (public) in Supabase Storage
  2. Run: python scripts/web/upload_figures_to_supabase.py
  3. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL in Vercel env vars

Local dev continues to work without Supabase: figures served from
disk via /api/figures as long as they exist locally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: remove EOL netcoreapp3.1 test invocation from build script

The test project (SqlClient.Tests.fsproj) targets net8.0 and net462 only;
it has never targeted netcoreapp3.1. Running dotnet test against a framework
the project does not target is a no-op at best and a spurious warning at
worst. .NET Core 3.1 reached end-of-life in December 2022.

Remove the dead invocation so the build script only tests against the
two supported frameworks: net462 (for .NET Framework) and net8.0 (for
modern .NET).

fix: commit figures so Vercel can serve them via /api/figures route

Previously all figures/**/*.png were gitignored, so Vercel deployed
the site without any figure files on disk, causing all <Image> elements
to show broken icons.

Changes:
- Remove figures/**/*.png|jpg|pdf|svg from .gitignore
- Keep ignoring large data files (*.nc, *.csv, *.parquet)
- Commit all existing figures in figures/cluster/, figures/main/,
  figures/exploratory/, figures/ep1_full/, figures/ep1_vertical/
- Also commit updated .gitignore exception for figures/ep_structure/

The /api/figures route reads files relative to the repo root via
process.cwd()/.., which works on Vercel because the full repo is
checked out and web/ is the Root Directory.

commit.message:
feat: build complete Vite + React 19 + TypeScript frontend for Surgery Planner

- Scaffold client with Vite, React 19, TypeScript
- Configure Tailwind CSS v4 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin
- Add path aliases (@/ and @surgeryplanner/shared)
- Create API client with JWT access token + refresh token support
- Add Zustand auth store with persistence
- Build UI primitives: Button, Input, Badge, Card, Skeleton, Dialog, Select, Tabs
- Build shared components: StatusBadge, StatsCard, EmptyState, ConfirmDialog,
  SearchInput, FormField, PageHeader, DataTable, FilterBar
- Build layout: AppShell, Sidebar (responsive), Topbar
- Build router with ProtectedRoute and browser history routing
- Build feature pages: Login, Dashboard, Templates, Blueprints, Settings
- Add vitest + @testing-library/react test suite (16 tests passing)
- Add eslint.config.js for server (pre-existing missing config)
